_note: DETERMINISTIC AWS-instance -> GCP machine-family map. Phase 2 MUST use this instead of a per-row 60/40 price-performance judgment, so the same AWS instance type ALWAYS maps to the same GCP family on every run. This is the single biggest source of run-to-run GCP-cost variance: when family choice is left to the model, c5.9xlarge maps to N2D on one run and N2 on the next, swinging the total by ~50%. validate_fix.py enforces this via the instance_family_mismatch gate.
_resolution_order: 1. arm64 / Graviton (*g/*gd/a1/t4g): -> C4A Axion (standard 4GB/vCPU for c/m Graviton; highmem 8GB/vCPU for r Graviton). C4A available in 28 regions incl. asia-south1. T2A is only 5 regions — use as last resort.
2. Burstable x86 (t2/t3/t3a): -> E2 (GCP cost-optimized burstable, no per-credit charge).
3. x86 memory-optimized >=6 GB/vCPU (r5/r6i/r6a/r7i/r7a): -> N2 (8 GB/vCPU). DO NOT use N2D (only 4 GB/vCPU) — it doubles vCPU count and inflates cost.
4. x86 ultra-memory >=12 GB/vCPU (x1e/x2i/u-*): -> M3 (16 GB/vCPU, available in major regions incl. asia-south1).
5. All other x86 (c/m general + compute-optimized, <=4 GB/vCPU): -> N2D AMD (best 60/40 price-performance, ~30% cheaper than N2).
